title: Learning while Deploying: Fleet-Scale Reinforcement Learning for Generalist Robot Policies
why: Fleet-scale offline-to-online RL framework for continual improvement of deployed VLA robot policies using autonomous rollouts and human interventions.

title: From Skill Text to Skill Structure: The Scheduling-Structural-Logical Representation for Agent Skills
why: Structured representation for reusable agent skills, improving skill discovery and risk assessment over text-only skill artifacts.

title: Map2World: Segment Map Conditioned Text to 3D World Generation
why: Generates controllable, scale-consistent 3D worlds that can support simulation and environment construction for embodied or autonomous agents.

title: Online Self-Calibration Against Hallucination in Vision-Language Models
why: Uses Monte Carlo Tree Search, reward mechanisms, and online preference optimization to reduce hallucination in multimodal models used by autonomous agents.

title: Learning to Act and Cooperate for Distributed Black-Box Consensus Optimization
why: LLM-guided framework for shaping internal actions and external cooperation patterns in distributed multi-agent optimization.
status: ok (reparsed with replacement for PDF surrogate text)

title: AnalogRetriever: Learning Cross-Modal Representations for Analog Circuit Retrieval
why: Cross-modal retrieval dataset and model integrated into the AnalogCoder agentic framework as a retrieval-augmented generation module.

title: Themis: Training Robust Multilingual Code Reward Models for Flexible Multi-Criteria Scoring
why: Introduces code reward-model benchmarks and a large preference dataset for multi-criteria scoring, useful for training and evaluating coding agents.
